doom.driver: Iran submits 14-point response to U.S. proposal to end war.
doom.blurb: IRAN BLINKS — BUT TRUST IS THE MISSING POINT
doom.summary: Iran has submitted a 14-point peace proposal, which sounds promising until you remember these are two sides that spent the last 65 days firing things at each other. Al Jazeera notes 'mistrust between the two sides is a big obstacle' — diplomatic understatement of the decade. Meanwhile NATO is quietly fraying: the U.S. is pulling 5,000 troops from Germany, Spain and Italy could be next, and two senior Republican senators are publicly alarmed — which means even the hawks are nervous.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran's 14-point proposal represents a potential off-ramp from the war, but deep mutual mistrust and ongoing Israel-Hezbollah exchanges keep the situation volatile.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: U.S. troop withdrawals from Germany — with Spain and Italy potentially next — are rattling NATO cohesion at a moment when European security architecture is under strain.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Kimi K2.6, an open-weights Chinese model, reportedly beat Claude, GPT-5.5, and Gemini in a coding benchmark, marking another frontier capability leap from a non-Western lab.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A second oil tanker hijacked near Yemen in 10 days and Somali pirates resurging suggest maritime chaos is quietly compounding the Hormuz crisis and global shipping risk.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: VS Code quietly inserting 'Co-Authored-by Copilot' into commits regardless of actual AI usage is a small but telling sign that AI provenance controls are already eroding in the wild.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, weather-dominated scene — a dense acrid smog haze swallows a Tehran boulevard at midday, the Alborz mountain foothills barely a ghost silhouette dissolved into charcoal murk behind layers of brown-yellow atmospheric haze, rain beginning to streak diagonally across the frame in thin dark crimson-tinted lines catching the oppressive filtered light — the entire scene color-graded in deep crimson and charcoal with firelight-orange undertones glowing through the smog layer — dominating the left half of the frame a massive propaganda billboard mounted on a Tehran intersection structure looms through the haze, its surface depicting a stylized flat-color map of the Strait of Hormuz rendered in high-contrast IRI propaganda aesthetic, the narrow waterway shown in steel-blue with silhouetted supertanker shapes and small aggressive speedboat forms clustered at the chokepoint — rain streaks run down the billboard face distorting the image — below the billboard Tehran street traffic moves ghostlike through the wet haze, Iranian-market Peugeot 405 sedans and weaving motorbikes reduced to dark shapes with amber headlights smearing through rain — in the right foreground a formal multi-page diplomatic document with an Islamic Republic tulip-and-sword emblem letterhead sits on a rain-wet surface, its pages curling at edges from moisture, 14 numbered lines barely visible — distant right background a carrier group silhouette sits at the horizon through impenetrable brown smog, massive gray hull shapes dissolving into atmosphere — no text, no antialiasing, 16-bit SNES pixel grid, dark crimson and charcoal palette throughout

dawn.driver: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner.
dawn.blurb: FIRST WOMAN TRAINS A DERBY WINNER
dawn.summary: In a sport steeped in 152 years of tradition, Cherie DeVaux just rewrote the record books — becoming the first woman ever to train a Kentucky Derby winner. On days when geopolitics feels relentlessly grim, sport hands us these clean, undeniable moments of historic first-ness. DeVaux didn't nudge open a door; she walked through one that had been closed for a century and a half, and Golden Tempo carried her there at 23-1 odds.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Cherie DeVaux's Derby win is the kind of barrier-breaking that outlasts the news cycle — it becomes the answer to a trivia question for generations.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Iran's submission of a formal 14-point peace proposal to the U.S. signals that diplomatic back-channels, however strained, remain open on day 65 of the war.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Scientists have discovered that boosting the Sox9 protein enables the brain's own support cells to clear Alzheimer's plaques — a promising endogenous therapy pathway.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: The Stillaguamish Tribe is purchasing and deliberately flooding former farmland to restore Chinook salmon habitat, a patient, long-horizon act of ecological repair.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Timmy the stranded humpback whale was successfully rescued after a weeks-long community effort that captured global attention — a small, warm proof that collective care works.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Extreme close-up filling the entire frame with a single red rose from the Derby blanket, its deep crimson petals rendered in rich saturated pixel clusters, dewdrops catching warm golden afternoon light in tiny glinting pixel squares. The rose dominates the composition completely, its velvety layers of petals built from careful dithering in scarlet, burgundy, and bright vermillion. At the very edges of the frame, implied environment bleeds in: the blurred pixel suggestion of chestnut horse coat in warm amber at the bottom border, a sliver of Churchill Downs twin spires rendered in soft ivory against a saturated emerald-gold sky in the upper corner, and at one petal's edge a ghostly reflection — a tiny dithered figure of a woman, arms raised, mouth open in joy, rendered in just enough pixels to read as pure triumphant emotion. The ground dust catches the low May sun, turning the background haze into liquid gold and emerald light. Every pixel of the rose glows as though lit from within, saturated and alive, the entire history of eight years crystallized in one perfect bloom.
